title: My Daily Routine
description: Describe your daily routine in Czech using reflexive verbs, morning and evening vocabulary, and time adverbs. Practice dialogues about daily schedules.

data.explanation: 'Vstávám' means 'I get up' or 'I am getting up'. It comes from the verb 'vstávat'.

data.sentence: Ráno ___ v sedm hodin, pak se sprchuju a ___.
data.explanation: 'Ráno vstávám v sedm hodin' (I get up at seven), 'pak se sprchuju' (then I shower), 'a snídám' (and I eat breakfast).

exercises.prompt: Translate into Czech: 'In the morning I get up at six o'clock.'

data.source: In the morning I get up at six o'clock.
data.grammar_note: Time in Czech uses 'v' + number + 'hodin'. 'Ráno' means 'in the morning'.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct time adverb

data.sentence: ___ pracuju v kanceláři. ___ večeřím doma s rodinou. ___ jdu spát.
data.explanation: 'Dopoledne' or 'odpoledne' (during the day) I work, 'večer' (in the evening) I eat dinner, 'v noci' (at night) I go to sleep.

exercises.prompt: Arrange words to form: 'In the evening I return home and have dinner.'

data.explanation: Czech: 'Večer se vracím domů a večeřím.' — 'Večer' (in the evening), 'se vracím' (I return), 'domů' (home), 'a večeřím' (and I have dinner).

exercises.answer_key: Večer se vracím domů a večeřím

exercises.prompt: Which time adverb means 'late at night'?

data.question_cz: Které příslovce času znamená 'late at night'?
data.question_en: Which time adverb means 'late at night'?
data.explanation: 'V noci' means 'at night' or 'during the night'. 'Ráno' = morning, 'odpoledne' = afternoon, 'večer' = evening.

exercises.prompt: Complete the dialogue about daily schedule

data.scenario: Two friends talking about their morning routines
data.explanation: 'Kdy vstáváš?' means 'When do you get up?'. Answer with 'Vstávám v sedm (hodin)' — 'I get up at seven (o'clock)'.

exercises.prompt: Translate into Czech: 'In the evening I go to bed at ten o'clock.'

data.source: In the evening I go to bed at ten o'clock.
accepted_answers: večer jdu spát v deset hodin
večer jdu spát v deset hodin.
večer chodím spát v deset hodin
data.grammar_note: 'Jdu spát' means 'I go to sleep'. 'Večer' means 'in the evening'. Use 'v' + number for time.

exercises.answer_key: večer jdu spát v deset hodin

data.explanation: Evening routine: 'Večer se sprchuju' (I shower in the evening) 'a pak se oblékám' (and then I get dressed). Both use reflexive 'se'.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear

data.expected_text: Ráno vstávám, sprchuju se a snídám
data.note: A typical morning routine sequence with reflexive verbs

exercises.answer_key: Ráno vstávám, sprchuju se a snídám

exercises.prompt: Say this daily routine phrase aloud

data.target_text: Každý den jdu do práce v osm hodin
data.translation_en: Every day I go to work at eight o'clock

exercises.answer_key: Každý den jdu do práce v osm hodin

exercises.prompt: Complete the dialogue about daily schedule

data.explanation: 'Co děláš odpoledne?' means 'What do you do in the afternoon?'. Answer: 'Vracím se domů a odpočívám' — 'I return home and relax'.
